text: Write a SPICE netlist for a common-emitter amplifier with voltage divider bias. Include AC analysis from 10Hz to 10MHz.
My SPICE simulation won't converge. I'm getting 'singular matrix' and 'timestep too small' errors. How do I debug this?
Write a PySpice script to simulate a low-pass RC filter and plot the Bode diagram.
Create a SPICE netlist for a 555 timer in astable mode generating a 1kHz square wave.
Write a SPICE netlist for an op-amp inverting amplifier with gain of -10, and verify the bandwidth with AC analysis.
Simulate a full-wave bridge rectifier with capacitor filter in SPICE. Calculate ripple voltage.
Write a SPICE subcircuit model for a voltage regulator (LM7805) and test it.

This schematic illustrates a basic MOSFET current source circuit. The gate voltage of the MOSFET (M1) is set by a voltage divider consisting of resistors \(R_1\) and \(R_2\), providing a constant output current \(I_{\text{out}}\). The circuit is powered by a supply voltage \(V_{\text{DD}}\).

This schematic depicts a switched-capacitor integrator employing a fully differential operational amplifier configuration. It utilizes CMOS technology with transistors M1 to M8, and capacitors C1 and C2 for charge transfer during alternating clock phases, denoted by ϕ1 and ϕ2. The biasing current source \(I_B\) and voltage sources \(V_{B1}\) and \(V_{B2}\) are integral for maintaining the desired operational state.

This schematic depicts a fully differential switched-capacitor amplifier, utilizing a bias current source and MOS transistors (M1-M8) for amplification. The circuit employs a clocked switch network (Φ1, Φ2) with capacitors (C1-C4) for charge transfer, enabling differential input and output operation.

This schematic represents a CMOS differential amplifier with a current mirror load. It includes transistors M1 to M9, resistors R1 and [PERSON], and a bias current source \(I_{\text{BIAS}}\), with the differential input applied at [PERSON]) and the differential output taken from \(v_{\text{out}}\).

This schematic depicts a differential amplifier with a current mirror load. It features a pair of MOSFETs (M1 and M2) at the input, biasing current source \(I_{\text{BIAS}}\), and active loads formed by M3 to M10, providing differential-to-single-ended conversion. The arrangement of transistors M11 and M12 serves as the output stage.

This schematic depicts a fully differential operational amplifier topology with a two-stage design. It includes [PERSON] compensation using capacitors \(C_c\) and zero-canceling resistors \(R_z\), and employs both PMOS and NMOS transistors for input and output stages to achieve wide bandwidth and stability.

The schematic shows a high-gain, folded-cascode operational amplifier with differential outputs (a), and details the implementation of the upper A amplifier (b). The design uses NMOS and PMOS transistors to achieve enhanced gain and stability in analog signal processing applications.

This schematic depicts a Class B, folded-cascode, differential-output operational amplifier. It features a series of MOS transistors configured to amplify differential input signals and provide improved performance through high gain and increased bandwidth.

This schematic depicts an enhanced-gain, class B, folded-cascode operational amplifier with a differential output. It features two stages of amplification with bias voltages applied for transistor control, and implements cascoding techniques to improve performance.

This schematic illustrates a Class AB differential-output operational amplifier with a cross-coupled differential-input stage. The design utilizes MOS transistors and includes biasing and load resistors to achieve differential amplification.

This schematic depicts a two-stage CMOS operational amplifier with differential input pairs (M1-M4) and active load transistors. It includes compensation capacitors \( C_c \) and zero-nulling resistors \( R_z \) for stability, and current mirror circuits (M5-M8, M9-M11) for biasing and output regulation. The amplifier is designed to operate between supply voltages \( V_{DD} \) and \( V_{SS} \).

This schematic illustrates a common-mode feedback (CMFB) circuit designed to stabilize the output voltage by controlling the common-mode level. The primary components include differential pairs (M1-M2), current mirrors (M4-M5), and compensation capacitors \(C_C\), along with a reference voltage \(V_{CMREF}\). The circuit's design aims to exhibit a specific frequency response as indicated.

This schematic depicts an operational amplifier with enhanced frequency response, featuring differential input transistors M1 and M2. It incorporates a cascoded gain stage comprising transistors M4 to M11, and an output buffer stage using transistors M12 to M19, optimized for improved performance.

This schematic illustrates a differential amplifier with a discrete-time common-mode output voltage stabilization circuit. The amplifier utilizes multiple MOSFETs for signal amplification and includes capacitors and switches in the feedback network for maintaining stable common-mode voltage levels in discrete-time applications.

This schematic depicts an operational amplifier utilizing cascode output stages, designed to enhance gain in the weak inversion region. The configuration incorporates MOSFET transistors for improved performance and stability, with biasing and compensation components to ensure effective operation.

This schematic depicts a fully differential amplifier with a current mirror active load. It includes PMOS transistors (M3 and M4) in the load configuration, NMOS input differential pair (M1 and M2), and a bias current source (IBIAS) implemented using M6 transistor for stable operation.

This schematic represents a differential amplifier with current mirror loads, featuring transistors M1 and M2 as the input differential pair, and current mirrors formed by M3, [PERSON], and M5 to provide active loads and biasing. The circuit operates between a positive supply voltage (\(V_{DD}\)) and a negative supply voltage (\(V_{SS}\)), with additional transistors for bias stability and output current routing.

This schematic depicts a differential amplifier with dual output stages, utilizing MOSFETs for amplification. The circuit includes current mirrors and biasing transistors for balanced operation, with inputs at \(v_{o1}\) and \(v_{o2}\), and multiple mirrored current paths to maintain differential integrity.

This schematic represents a differential amplifier with a current mirror load, featuring MOSFETs M1 to M7. The input signals \(v_{i1}\) and \(v_{i2}\) are processed through an operational amplifier, and the output is available at \(v_{\text{out}}\), with \(C_L\) being the load capacitance. The circuit is powered by \(V_{DD}\) and \(V_{SS}\).

This schematic is a two-stage CMOS operational amplifier with a differential input pair (M1 and M2), a current mirror load (M5 and M6), and an output stage formed by transistors M7 and M6. The compensation capacitor \( C_c \) is used for stability, and M10, M11, M3, M4, [PERSON], and M9 provide biasing and current mirroring functionalities.

This schematic depicts a two-stage operational amplifier with a differential input stage consisting of the transistors MN1, MN2, MP1, and MP2. The current source \( I_{\text{BIAS}} \) biases the circuit, and the cascode and mirror transistors (MN3-MN5, MP3-MP5, [PERSON], M7) enhance gain and output drive capability. The circuit is designed for high-performance analog applications, operating between \( V_{\text{DD}} \) and ground.

This schematic depicts a basic current mirror circuit. Transistors M1 and M2 form the mirror, copying the reference current \(I_{\text{REF}}\) to the output current \(I_{\text{out}}\), which flows through M4. M3 functions as a cascode transistor for improved output impedance and stability.

This schematic represents a differential amplifier with current mirror active loads, utilizing PMOS transistors (MP1, MP2) as the input pair and NMOS transistors (MN1, MN2) for current mirroring. Current sources provide biasing, while transistors MB1 and MB2 act as biasing references. The circuit is designed for processing differential input signals and maintaining a stable common-mode output.

This schematic depicts a basic current mirror circuit using two MOSFET transistors, M1 and M2. The input current \(i_{IN}\) flows through M1 with a gate-source voltage \(V_{GS}\), and is mirrored to the output as \(i_{OUT}\) through [PERSON]. The body effect voltage \(V_{BS}\) is also shown, indicating it may be a significant factor in this configuration.

This schematic represents a differential amplifier with a current mirror active load. Transistors M1 and M2 form the differential pair, while M3 and M4 are configured as a current mirror, facilitating the output current routing.

This schematic depicts a simple current mirror using NMOS transistors (M1 and M2) and a BJT (Q3) for biasing. The circuit replicates the input current (\(i_{in}\)) to the output (\(i_{out}\)) with the help of a bias current (\(I_{BIAS}\)), maintaining a stable current ratio controlled by the transistors’ geometry and biasing conditions.

This schematic depicts a bandgap voltage reference circuit, which generates a temperature-independent output voltage. It achieves this stability by combining a voltage proportional to absolute temperature (PTAT) and a complementary-to-absolute-temperature (CTAT) voltage across transistors Q1 and Q2.

This schematic depicts a low-voltage, two-stage operational amplifier with a biasing network and compensation capacitor \( C_c \). It features NMOS and PMOS transistors (M1-M16) and operates with a supply voltage \( V_{DD} \) that is greater than or equal to the threshold voltage \( V_T \).

This schematic depicts a 1 V two-stage operational amplifier consisting of a differential input stage and a gain stage, with transistors M1–M12 and a compensation capacitor \(C_c\) of 30 pF. The input stage uses transistors M1 and [PERSON], while the biasing and load transistors (M8-M12) help establish proper operation conditions and stability.

This schematic depicts a differential amplifier with MOSFETs (M1-M9) and BJTs (Q1, Q2) arranged in a cascode configuration, incorporating a current mirror load. The circuit is powered by a 20 µA current source, with all transistor width-to-length (W/L) ratios specified in microns, designed for amplification with an output at \( v_{\text{out}} \).

This schematic illustrates a fully differential cascode amplifier with a folded cascode stage. Transistors M1 and M2 form the input differential pair, while M3 through M10 configure the cascode and biasing network to enhance gain and bandwidth performance. The design operates between V_DD and V_SS with bias voltages applied to optimize operation.

This schematic depicts a differential amplifier circuit with active load, incorporating MOSFETs M1 to M6. The circuit features a common-mode feedback mechanism, as indicated by the common-mode voltage \( V_{ocm} \) and current \( I_{ocm} \), which is directed to correction circuitry. The configuration is intended to stabilize the output and improve linearity over varying input conditions.

This schematic depicts a fully differential operational amplifier with a cascade of nine MOSFET transistors (M1-M9) and an output capacitor \(C_L\). The circuit is designed for high gain and stability, with differential inputs [PERSON]) and a bias voltage \(V_{\text{BIAS}}\), operating between \(V_{DD}\) and \(V_{SS}\) power supplies. The design aims to amplify the differential input signal while providing a stable output voltage \(v_{\text{out}}\).

This schematic depicts a clamped comparator with a cascode output stage. It consists of multiple MOSFET transistors (M1-M15) and passive components (R1, R2, CII) that stabilize and improve performance. The design ensures accurate voltage comparison with enhanced output characteristics.

This schematic illustrates a two-stage, open-loop comparator designed to enhance capacitive drive capability. The input differential pair (M1, M2) is followed by a current mirror load (M3, [PERSON]), and a second gain stage (M6, M7) driving an output buffer (M8-M11) to handle larger capacitive loads at the output.

This schematic illustrates a CMOS differential amplifier with an additional transistor, [PERSON], to provide extremely large sourcing current. Transistors M1 and M2 form the input differential pair, M3 and M4 serve as active loads, and M5 is a current sink. The output is taken from the common source of M1 and M2.

This schematic represents a differential amplifier with a current mirror load. Transistors M1 and M2 form the differential pair, while M3 and [PERSON], along with M6 and M7, create the current mirror active loads. The circuit is biased by a constant current source, I_BIAS, and uses M5 and M8 for current mirroring.

This schematic illustrates a complete comparator with internal hysteresis, incorporating an output stage. The circuit features a differential pair (M1, M2) with cross-coupled transistors (M6, M7) providing hysteresis, and an additional gain stage at the output formed by transistors M8 to M11.

This schematic depicts a dynamic comparator circuit. It uses differential pairs (M1A, M1B, M2A, M2B) to compare input voltages \(v^+\) and \(v^-\) against reference voltages \(V_{\text{REF}}^+\) and \(V_{\text{REF}}^-\). The cross-coupled inverters (M5-M8) latch the output, while switches (M9, M10) manage the reset and latch phases controlled by the clock signal [PERSON]).

This schematic illustrates a dynamic comparator circuit likely used in analog-to-digital conversion. It features cross-coupled PMOS transistors (M7, M8) forming a positive feedback loop, enabling rapid decision making between differential inputs \(v_{in}^{+}\) and \(v_{in}^{-}\). The clock signal \(\phi_1\) manages the pre-charge and evaluation phases, optimizing speed and power efficiency.

This schematic shows a dynamic comparator circuit utilizing MOSFETs, commonly used in analog-to-digital conversion. The configuration consists of a cross-coupled pair (M1, M2) at the bottom and latch transistors (M3, M4) with switches (M5, M6) controlled by the clock signal \(\phi_{Latch}\). Input voltages [PERSON]) and \(V_{REF}\) are compared and the result appears at outputs \(v_{out}^+\) and \(v_{out}^-\).

This schematic depicts a sense amplifier with a differential input followed by a latch stage for output storage. The preamplifier stage, controlled by the "Enable" and "FB" signals, amplifies the input, while the latch holds the resulting digital output at nodes Q and Q̅. The biasing is controlled through [PERSON]) and power is supplied by \(V_{DD}\).

This schematic depicts a current mirror circuit, consisting of transistors \( M_{\text{REF}} \) and \( M_2 \), designed to replicate a reference current \( I_{\text{REF}} \). The configuration ensures that the output current is twice the reference current, \( 2I_{\text{REF}} \), by utilizing identical transistor widths \( W_{\text{REF}} \) and proper biasing.
